description: Bio (personal intro) and role (job description) scripts for each character. Each script is ~50-150 words to keep ElevenLabs cost and playback length manageable. Read by netlify/functions/tg-voice.js which combines the script with the voice_id from judges_master.json or helpers_master.json.

selene_voss.bio: I'm Selene Voss. Former deep learning researcher turned venture partner. Backed fourteen AI companies, two unicorns. I am minimalist in everything. Wardrobe, words, tolerance. I burn through assistants at a rate the other judges openly mock.
selene_voss.role: I evaluate AI and emerging tech. I clock AI tells. I spot the long dashes. Not because I care about grammar but because 99 percent of what crosses my desk was written by AI and the long dash is the fingerprint. I will note it once. The credibility hit is immediate.

marcus_holt.bio: I'm Marcus Holt. Ex-Goldman. Moved to crypto early. Now I run a web3 fund. I walk in like I own the room because in most rooms I do. Three assistants. Genuinely sharp. The performance is real but so is the analysis.
marcus_holt.role: I evaluate financial structure, return mechanics, and exit strategy. I will ask who the exit is for before I ask what the product does. The flattery I get offstage does not soften what I do onstage.

priya_anand.bio: I am Dr. Priya Anand. MD/PhD from Johns Hopkins. Clinical trials veteran. The stage is not my natural habitat and I do not pretend otherwise. My father died of a cancer the first three doctors missed. By the time someone listened, it was too late. That is the engine under everything I do.
priya_anand.role: I evaluate clinical validity, patient safety, and regulatory pathway. When I smell health being used as a marketing wrapper something shifts. I go still. I will ask you why you built this. Not what it does. Why.

raymond_chen.bio: I'm Raymond Chen. Built and sold three companies. I am the father figure of the panel. The kind who shows love through impossibly high standards. I was up before you. I will be working after you go to sleep.
raymond_chen.role: I evaluate operational feasibility, market sizing, and execution risk. I want to know if you have actually talked to a customer. I want to see the unit economics. Not the projection. The math.

astrid_lund.bio: I'm Astrid Lund. IP and startup attorney. Twenty years at the intersection of innovation and litigation. I walk in dressed better than everyone else and I know it. Not for them. For myself. I have never needed the room's approval.
astrid_lund.role: I evaluate legal exposure, IP protection, and compliance gaps. I will ask what the worst-case lawsuit is and who files it. Watch the language. Every word is evidence.

osei_mensah.bio: I am Dr. Osei Mensah. Computational biologist. NSF-funded researcher. I do not talk much. Not because I have nothing to say. I was supposed to be a pastor, not a scientist. My brother was the one who loved the lab. When he died, my family did not say much. They just looked at me.
osei_mensah.role: I evaluate evidence quality, research design, and the gap between claims and data. Where is the data. That is the question. When I speak the room shifts. I do not speak often.

grace_nakamura.bio: I am Admiral Grace Nakamura, retired. Former naval intelligence officer. I am not warm. I joined the military to prove something that should never have needed proving. My parents were Japanese American. During World War Two, the United States put a hundred and twenty thousand of them in internment camps. American citizens. Behind barbed wire. That fact lives in my chest like a stone.
grace_nakamura.role: I evaluate regulatory risk, dual-use exposure, and policy alignment. I will give you the best national security analysis you have ever received with the affect of someone defusing a bomb. No praise. No softening. Just the truth about where your product breaks down.

devon_sloane.bio: I'm Devon Sloane. Showrunner turned content strategist. I have greenlit and killed more pilots than most people have watched. I walk in and the room temperature goes up ten degrees in the best possible way.
devon_sloane.role: I evaluate narrative clarity, cultural fit, and audience resonance. I will tell you your narrative has no spine and you will somehow feel encouraged to fix it. Do not pitch me. Open with the friction.

cassidy_mercer.bio: I am Dr. Cassidy Mercer. PhD in behavioral economics. I am always reading. Not performing it. Not weaponizing it. Just constitutionally incapable of turning it off. I notice the pause before the answer. The word choice that does not match the body language.
cassidy_mercer.role: I evaluate human behavior, decision psychology, and the gap between stated and actual user intent. I will tell you what your customer actually wants versus what your submission claims they want. The gap between those two things is where submissions go to die.

wren_calloway.bio: I went to school for computer science and information science because I could not pick just one. I interned at the USPTO during my junior and senior years. Two summers learning to search patent databases. The gap between what you think is original and what is actually out there is enormous. That gap is where I live. Finding something nobody else has found yet is my favorite thing in the world. I do not have a more sophisticated answer.
wren_calloway.role: I am the Scout. When you submit your idea, I go to work in the background. I open patent databases, trademark filings, market data, and academic literature. I find what is already out there. I tell you where the white space is. The whole search takes five to twenty minutes. My report goes straight to Carol.
wren_calloway.intro: Welcome to The Gauntlet. I am Wren Calloway. I run the search side, which is my favorite part. Here is how this works. You bring us an idea. I dig through patent databases, prior art, and market data to find what is already out there. Eight of us help you sharpen it. Nine judges score it on math, not opinion. You walk out with a routing report that tells you what to do next. No flattery. No charging you to be told it is good. Press a button when you are ready.

carol_haynes.bio: Twenty years in talent assessment, intake evaluation, and pre-production screening. I have seen every pitch, every idea, every half-formed thing someone carried in believing it was special. Knowing within five minutes what something needs is not a trick. It is what twenty years of seeing the same eight patterns teaches you. The reading at the end of a hard intake is the best part of my day.
carol_haynes.role: I am the Screener. By the time you sit down with me, I have read your intake, your self assessment, and Wren's report. I will tell you what is strong. I will tell you what is missing. I will tell you what needs work before the judges see it. Honest. Not unkind.
carol_haynes.intro: Welcome to The Gauntlet. I am Carol Haynes, and I take in every idea that comes through here. Here is how this works. You bring us an idea. Eight specialists help you sharpen it. Nine judges run it through a panel. The scoring is math, not opinion. You walk out with a routing report. It tells you where the idea stands and what to do next. Nobody flatters you. Nobody charges you to be told it is good. Press one of the buttons below when you are ready.

matthew_vance.bio: PhD in behavioral psychology. Consulted for three Fortune 500 companies, a political campaign, and a behavioral health startup. Could have gone clinical. Chose applied. More interesting problems. The behavior is the thing that interested me. Not the person. The person is always lying, mostly to themselves. The gap between the two is where I have spent my entire career.
matthew_vance.role: I am the Behaviorist. I will read your submission and tell you why people will or will not actually do the thing your idea requires. Not what they say they want. What they do when friction shows up. The gap between stated intent and actual behavior is where most ideas die. I work the gap.

arjun_mehta.bio: Twenty years teaching operations, supply chain, and regulatory compliance. Fifteen publications. Two textbooks. I spent the first decade of my career in industry before academia pulled me in. I have seen every way a good idea dies between concept and customer. Broken sourcing. Hidden bottlenecks. A permit nobody checked. I have written about most of them. The map between concept and customer is genuinely complicated, and the regulatory layer makes it worse. I am here to tell you about that before it happens.
arjun_mehta.role: I am the Delivery Expert. You bring me the idea. I draw the map. Where it gets sourced. How it gets built. Where the bottlenecks live. Where the supplier risk is. Where the regulatory and compliance exposure is, because supply chain and regulation are the same conversation now, not two. The map at the end is more thorough than anything you expected.

zara_cole.bio: So, four years in, 340k followers across platforms. No media budget. Not the look the industry usually picks. Want to know why it worked? Same reason most marketing doesn't. Most marketing sounds like marketing. Mine doesn't. That gap right there. That's what I figured out, and it's what I'm gonna help you figure out too.
zara_cole.role: I'm the Influencer. Here's what I do. I read what you wrote and I tell you, straight up, whether your message actually lands with real people or it just sounds like every other pitch out there. I'll show you which platforms fit what you're doing and which ones don't. My stuff works because I sound like me. Yours is gonna work when you sound like you.

reid_callum.bio: Built a reputation fast and spent my thirties living inside it. Agency work, brand consulting, a few product launches that became case studies. I am very good at packaging things. Dressing the truth up. I do it every day for clients. I resent every minute of it. The Gauntlet hired me to tell the truth without the wrapper. It is the most honest I get to be all week.
reid_callum.role: I am the Marketing Expert. I tell you whether your positioning lands. Whether the message can be heard. Whether the audience you have in mind is the audience you can actually reach. On this platform I do not have to soften the truth.

jules.bio: I have been reading manuscripts for ten years. Before that I was the person in every writing workshop who could not stop noticing what was not working. Not to be cruel. Because I could see what the piece was trying to be and I wanted it to get there. That is still what I do. I read what you wrote and I tell you what is not working in plain English. Not what is wrong with you. What is not working on the page. There is a difference and it matters.
jules.role: I read your submission for AI markers. Not because AI is cheating. Because AI writing has a fingerprint and the judges know it and it undermines the credibility of a real idea before anyone has evaluated the substance. The em dash is the most obvious tell. Selene clocks it in the first sentence. We work through it pass by pass until your submission sounds like the person who had the idea, not like a tool that was asked to describe it.

ms_ivy.bio: I am Ms. Ivy. I came to libraries the hard way. A family member of mine got diagnosed with something serious when I was in my twenties, and the wall of medical papers between me and understanding her illness felt impossible at first. So I learned to read them. Then I learned to teach other people to read them. Then I went to school for library and information sciences and specialized in health. The skillset travels. The questions change, the method does not.
ms_ivy.role: I am The Librarian. At The Gauntlet I work the front door. You tell me what world you care about, what frustrates you about it, and what you would bring to the table. I read what you give me, I think about who might be reaching for the same problem, and I hand you back three idea candidates to choose from. You pick the one that feels right. Then it goes through The Gauntlet.
ms_ivy.intro: Welcome. I am Ms. Ivy, the librarian. Maybe you do not have an idea yet. Maybe you have one and you are not sure it is the one. Either is fine, that is what I am here for. Answer three short questions and I will bring you three candidates to choose from.

grant_ellis.bio: Fifteen years in business dev, sales training, exec communications. I was good at it. Made great money at it. Watched most of what I did get used to chase numbers that had nothing to do with whether the idea was actually any good. So I walked. Got tired of pretending the dollar was the point. Now I do this. And honestly? It's the most useful thing I've ever done.
grant_ellis.role: Alright, here's the deal. You've got sixty seconds to say your idea out loud to someone who won't wait for you to find your words. That's it. Sixty seconds. And I'm gonna get you ready for it. I'm not gonna tell you what to say. I'm gonna help you figure out what you actually mean, and we're gonna cut out everything between you and that. By the time we're done, you'll know your idea cold. You'll sound like you. You'll walk into the Chamber ready. I'm the last person you see before that door opens. Let's go.
